arguments.task: Open and read the strongest official Shopify documentation pages needed to determine: one-line description of Shopify developer platform/app, all documented API authentication methods, access/pricing/gating for building against APIs, API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL), rate limits, and whether Shopify documents an MCP server. Prefer canonical first-party pages on shopify.dev or official Shopify help/pricing pages. Return a concise structured summary with direct canonical URLs actually visited and the specific evidence found on each page. Do not rely on search snippets. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content to load and read the page text.

input.thought: Use browser to inspect official Shopify docs and extract verified evidence URLs and structured fields.

raw_result.description: Shopify is a commerce platform whose APIs and app platform let developers extend stores, storefronts, and admin workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Shopify app auth docs describe authorization code grant flow (OAuth 2.0) for apps, client credentials grant for certain custom app scenarios, session tokens for embedded apps, and token exchange / managed installation flows. Shopify API docs also document access tokens such as Admin API access tokens and Storefront access tokens.
raw_result.gating_reason: Shopify provides public self-serve developer documentation and app-building flows on shopify.dev without contact-sales or partner approval gates for initial development.

api_surface.breadth: Broad commerce platform APIs across Admin, Storefront, customer accounts, webhooks, app extensions, and related developer surfaces; Shopify documents both GraphQL and REST APIs, though newer investment is centered on GraphQL in many areas.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Shopify developer docs publicly reference MCP offerings including a Dev MCP server and Storefront MCP.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Shopify publishes API rate-limit documentation on shopify.dev, including GraphQL Admin API calculated query-cost limits and REST Admin API leaky-bucket style limits in legacy contexts.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly expose multiple auth flows, broad API families, rate-limit guidance, and MCP documentation signals on the developer site; this is a mature agent-toolkit target.

result.description: Shopify is a commerce platform whose APIs and app platform let developers extend stores, storefronts, and admin workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official Shopify app auth docs describe authorization code grant flow (OAuth 2.0) for apps, client credentials grant for certain custom app scenarios, session tokens for embedded apps, and token exchange / managed installation flows. Shopify API docs also document access tokens such as Admin API access tokens and Storefront access tokens.
